Hey, what's going on? Okay, so just wanted to give you a little bit of kind of a heads up here The energy that you have in the room is gonna be really important for how you Or to determine whether or not you're gonna close well in a group setting That's first thing is like your energy and you got to be like you got it like because you're spreading your energy out You need to be almost over the top energy is in order for everyone to be impacted by what you're giving off So that's first thing super high energy super positive super just like even people coming in like I couldn't find you're like Well, I'm glad we give you a workout already You know like just kind of have that overwhelmingly positive energy that no one can take down First thing second thing Remember why you're doing this you're trying to help these people and we're trying to do everything that we can to Create a compact like basically explain to them why they don't do this the likelihood that they will succeed is really Well, they like you have to come in with it with that intention in order to really get The sales right third thing is you need to have a very very strong presence I said earlier energy, but like you need to control the room Like you can't let some loud person can't let some sort of like often do try and control or start asking your interrupting Like you can't do that like you have to take control Otherwise it would totally unravel it from you in your sales, and I don't want that to happen the fourth thing and this is kind of the last thing is that You need to go into this with a mindset of practice, okay? Group sales is another skill all together I talked to Russell about this Not that long ago, and he was like man It's like I wish I could sell and I'm like dude you sell all the time but he was like no pitching and selling the way you sell are totally different skills and I have I have I Don't say I disagree But like I think there's a lot of truth to that is it's just a different skill set It's still selling it's just it's just another skill set and learning how to make presentations that are persuasive Especially live so I just want you to like just give yourself a break if the first couple don't go perfectly And if they do awesome, I hope you knock it out of the park. That's the point right? but just Be willing to learn just like your first few sales the only thing is that stakes are a little bit higher because you got more people That's fair, but um Ultimately if you're moving your way down the building you have to match to the skill so You just need to get better because if you can do that and you can transfer the skill to a sales manager All of a sudden like I said earlier 80 hours a week becomes like five to ten and then that is a very scalable sales model So I just want you to like I don't like your first day like man I want two for you know two for eight or whatever In one orientation like it happens you'll get better at it And then over time it gets really like sometimes you actually close higher percentages in group because you have social That means that you can use scarcity and urgency and social proof that can actually That can they can move in the right way kind of move the crowd So and you'll get better at it the nuances a little jokes that you'll have and you'll make the picture of that pitch sounds like me And so you'll edit little pieces, but that framework that I'm talking about the belief breaking this what's really important What's underlying but if you want to put your own flavor on it by all means you're supposed to so anyways I just want to preface that before we get into the down dirty
